2603.17808.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
2603.17808.paper_summary: Video generative models are increasingly used as world models for robotics, where a model generates a future visual rollout conditioned on the current observation and task instruction, and an inverse dynamics model (IDM) converts the generated frames into executable robot actions. However, current video world models lack explicit executability constraints. As a result, visually coherent rollouts may still violate rigid-body and kinematic consistency, producing unstable or infeasible control commands when decoded by an IDM. We refer to this mismatch between visual generation and physically executable control as the executability gap. While this gap can be mitigated at inference time using techniques such as rejection sampling, such approaches are inefficient due to the high cost of video generation. In this paper, we leverage the executability gap as a training signal and introduce Executable Video Alignment (EVA), a reinforcement-learning post-training framework for aligning video world models. EVA trains an inverse dynamics model on real robot trajectories and repurposes it as a reward model that evaluates generated videos through the action sequences they induce, encouraging smooth motions measured by velocity, acceleration, and jerk while penalizing actions that violate embodiment constraints. Importantly, the reward remains informative even when generated videos contain severe visual artifacts, since such artifacts typically translate into unstable or out-of-bound actions. Experiments on the RoboTwin benchmark and a real bimanual robot show that EVA reduces embodiment-specific artifacts in generated rollouts and improves downstream task execution success.
2603.17808.paper_summary_zh: 視頻生成模型越來越多地被用作機器人的世界模型，其中模型根據當前觀察和任務指令生成未來的視覺展開，而反向動力學模型（IDM）將生成的幀轉換為可執行的機器人動作。<lb/>然而，目前的視頻世界模型缺乏明確的可執行性約束。<lb/>因此，視覺上連貫的展開仍可能違反剛體和運動學的一致性，當由IDM解碼時，會產生不穩定或不可行的控制命令。<lb/>我們將視覺生成和物理可執行控制之間的這種不匹配稱為可執行性差距。<lb/>雖然可以在推理時使用拒絕採樣等技術來減輕這一差距，但由於視頻生成的高成本，這些方法效率低下。<lb/>在本文中，我們利用可執行性差距作為訓練信號，並引入可執行視頻對齊（EVA），這是一種用於對齊視頻世界模型的強化學習後訓練框架。<lb/>EVA在真實機器人軌跡上訓練反向動力學模型，並將其重新用作獎勵模型，通過其引發的動作序列來評估生成的視頻，鼓勵通過速度、加速度和抖動測量的平滑運動，同時懲罰違反具身約束的動作。<lb/>重要的是，即使生成的視頻包含嚴重的視覺伪影，獎勵仍然保持信息性，因為這些伪影通常會轉化為不穩定或超出範圍的動作。<lb/>在RoboTwin基準和一個真實的雙手機器人上的實驗表明，EVA減少了生成展開中的具身特定伪影，並改善了下游任務執行的成功率。
2603.17808.author: Ruixiang Wang et.al.
2603.17808.authors: Ruixiang Wang, Qingming Liu, Yueci Deng, Guiliang Liu, Zhen Liu, Kui Jia
